root-question.text: Does the task have a deterministic success signal (test command, schema validator, rule check)?

question.text: Are subtasks enumerable up-front from the brief alone?
Are all tool actions reversible (no prod-state mutation)?

branch.conclusion: Use Plan-and-Execute. Enumerable subtasks + success signal + reversible. Cap max_steps=8.
Escalate. Irreversible actions never go through autonomous loops.

question.text: Is the success check stable (same input → same verdict)?

branch.conclusion: Use Reflexion. Stable check + unknown subtasks = retry-with-critique pattern.
Use ReAct. Reflexion without a stable check turns into max_attempts every run.

question.text: Are tool actions reversible (read-only or scratch dirs)?

branch.conclusion: Use ReAct. No success signal → cannot use Reflexion; reversible actions tolerate exploration. Cap max_iterations=10.
Escalate. No success signal + non-reversible actions = no agent pattern fits.

example.p: Brief: "Find three papers on agent harness engineering." has_success_signal=false → q5 → action_reversibility=reversible (web search) → conclusion: ReAct.
Brief: "Fix failing pytest." has_success_signal=true → q2 → subtasks_enumerable=false → q4 → success_check_stable=true (pytest is deterministic) → conclusion: Reflexion.
